column2: Care and Support Services (CASS) on Call
column4: CASS on Call is a 24/7/365 phone line through the University of Virginia’s Care and Support Services that offers urgent, non-clinical consultation and referrals for students’ safety, well-being, and urgent needs that can’t wait until the next business day. It connects students with appropriate resources for issues like significant illness, urgent mental health concerns, safety resources, emergency housing, or engaging with emergency contacts.
column7: Are you struggling with homesickness? You're not alone. Check out If You're Reading This UVA, a website where UVA students share challenges they've faced and offer words of support.
column10: Student Health and Wellness offers financial resources and tips that can help you improve your financial security, such as budgeting, emergency funds, and reducing expenses.
column13: Free, confidential consults with a CAPS therapist are available globally for UVA Students who have non-emergency concerns. Please reach out for help if you are struggling.
column16: Visit Student Health, located at 550 Brandon Avenue, for all of your medical, laboratory, and pharmacy needs.
column19: UVA has 1,000+ student organizations that you can be a part of. Hoos involved helps students find organizations and events that are present on and around Grounds. Get involved today!
column22: Friendships you make in UVA clubs and organizations may lead to something more! Sign up, help out, and meet your community!

column4: Don't stay stuck working on a paper! Reach out to a consultant at the UVA Writing Center.
column7: Unsure of how to cope with homesickness? There is a step-by-step guide from CAPS at Kansas University that can help you manage these feelings as you navigate college.
column10: Looking to apply for a scholarship or grant? Student Financial Services provides information on the application process for scholarships and grants funded by and outside of the university and how they affect your financial aid.
column13: Free scheduled telehealth visits with a mental health provider for UVA students. Access 24/7 virtual care from anywhere!
column16: WahooWell is a positive, free, confidential, and non-judgmental resource. You will meet with a well-being facilitator who will help you accomplish personally meaningful goals regarding stress, sleep, and social health.
column19: Lean on Me provides peer-to-peer text lines that allow for easy access to confidential non-crisis support. Anytime you feel lonely and need someone to talk to, Lean on Me will be there at 434-300-3434.
column22: The Women's Center of Virginia offers individual as well as couples counseling that is empowerment-based and derived from evidence-based approaches.

column4: The UVA Career Center houses information, tips, and job or internship ideas depending on your chosen career.
column5: CAPS Group Therapy: Interpersonal Process Group
column7: Having issues with family? This group helps students to understand their identity and self-worth while also learning how to improve communication with others.
column10: If you are facing unexpected financial hardships, you can apply for a one-time grant through the UVA Parents Rainy Day Fund to help cover travel expenses, tuition payments, and more.
column13: HELP Line is a free, confidential, 24/7 telephone hotline serving Albemarle County. Volunteers can provide judgement-free listening and help refer you to long-term services. You can reach them at 434-924-TALK.
column16: PHEs are trained undergraduate students who share their knowledge of  health and wellness topics with residence halls, fraternity/sororities (FOA approved), and student groups. They also offer one-on-one health consultations.
column19: WahooWell is a positive, free, confidential, and non-judgmental resource. You will meet with a well-being facilitator who will help you accomplish personally meaningful goals regarding stress, sleep, and social health.
column22: Have you recently gone through a break-up? The Jed Foundation is a non-profit that helps young people cope with challenges, such as break-ups, and manage their emotional health.

column4: From academic caldenars calendars to university regulations, The Undergraduate Student Record provides all the information you need to know about being an undergraduate student at UVA.
column7: The Better Help Channel provides resources to help people maintain their health and well-being, including how to navigate conflict that might arise within your family.
column10: If you experiecing a financial emergency, you can apply for an Honor Loan or an SFS Emergency Loan through ODOS.
column13: If You're Reading This UVA is a mental health resource where students, faculty, and alumni share their journeys and offer words of support. You can also read letters from other universities and submit your own story.
column16: Mindfulness meditiation improves your physical health and wellbeing. Guided meditations offered by the Mindfulness Center here at UVA may help you!
column19: Housed on the third floor of Newcomb Hall, the LGBTQ Center is a physical space and programming center that raises awareness and promotes inclusion for individuals who identidy as LGBTQ and allies.
column22: Available on Spotify and Apple Podcasts, Small Things Often offers research-based tips to help you improve your relationship every Monday and Wednesday mornings.

column4: The Student Disability Access Center (SDAC) provides students with accomodations to ensure that all students can participate in opportunities around Grounds.
column7: Mental Health America is a non-profit whose mission is to to promote mental health. Among other things, MHA offers tips on how you can talk to your loved ones about mental health.
column10: ODOS has partnered with the Career Center to help students find employment opportunities on- and off-grounds. Check out listings that may be the right fit for you!
column13: Don't be afraid to reach out to CAPS for free, individualized therapy.
column16: The Aquatic Fitness Center, located on 450 Whitehead Rd, offers an Olympic sized pool, fitness classes, and many other opportunities to engage in physical exercise.
column19: Join the Queer Student Union to be educated, empowered, and connected with other members through a range of programming and advocacy work.
column22: Available on iTunes and the Google Play store, the Gottman Institute Card Decks App offers research-based questions, statements, and ideas for improving your relationship.

column4: Deciding what classes to enroll in next semester? Made for UVA students, Hoos' List gives you an extensive list of class options.
column5: Hoos First - Support for First-Gen Students
column7: From the Office of the Dean of Students (ODOS), Hoos First offers support for first-generation students through programming, social events, and building a network of connection.
column10: The UVA Community Food Pantry, now located in Newcomb Hall, is open to all students during SAC hours (7am-12am Monday through Thursday & Saturday, 7am-9pm Friday, 8am-12am Sunday).
column13: CAPS offers multiple group options, such as skills groups, support groups, and interpersonal process groups that can  help you tackle challenging subjects while building bonds with community members.
column16: Looking to incorporate physical activity into your day? There are a lot of places to walk around Grounds, including the McCormick Dorms Loop (.6 mi), Old Ivy Trail (.8 mi), West Lawn Loop (.4 mi), and more!
column17: Multicultural Student Services Advising and Personal Support
column19: Multicultural Student Services (MSS) at the University of Virginia supports intercultural engagement and student success by offering programs, support, and community-building opportunities through several identity-focused centers—including the Multicultural Student Center, LGBTQ Center, Latinx Student Center, Interfaith Student Center, and Asian American Student Center—open to all students.
column22: Using your answers to questions and a computer science algorithm, The Marriage Pact at UVA will try to find your optimal match on grounds! It works to address loneliness by matching users with other students similar to them -- and they say romance is dead!

column4: Deciding what classes to enroll in next semester? Created by and for UVA students, The Course Forum allows students to browse potential courses and read other students' reviews.
column5: First Generation Low Income Student Support
column7: From the Office of the Dean of Students (ODOS), First Generation Low Income Student Support promotes a sense of belonging, community, and connection for first-generation, low income, and military-affiliated/veteran students through programs such as one-on-one meetings, panels, and financial assistance.
column10: The Women's Center has a food pantry! It is located on the main floor of the building and is open from 9am-5pm Monday, Thursday, and Friday and 9am-8pm Tuesday and Wednesday during the academic year. As well as food, the pantry also provides basic hygiene products.
column11: Student Health and Wellness - Care Management
column13: Not sure where to start? Questions about insurance? A CAPS Care Manager can help you get started and access mental health care both on- and off- Grounds.
column16: Physical exercise is important. UVA Intramurals offers many opportunities to get involved in an intramural sport or recreation that you enjoy, such as tennis, volleyball, and more!
column19: Feeling lonely is more common than you think. Get to know other students while earning credit with Hoos Connected, a program that brings together 8-10 students to discuss what makes meaningful connections.
column22: Through CAPS, the LGBTQ+ Support Group is a space where students can explore their gender and sexuality and is focused on fostering healthier adjustment and self-development.

column4: Handshake connectes students directly with potential employers and offers resources for job-hunting students, including Career Counseling appointments that can strengthen your academic and career path.
column5: UVA International Students and Scholars Program
column7: Do you have questions about being an international student? The International Students and Scholars Program, a division of the International Studies Office, offers cultural, social, educational, and legal resources that help meet the unique needs of international students.
column10: Search here to find Virginia's food stamp offices.
column13: Through UVA's Medical School, the Mindfulness Center offers online classes, events, recordings, and resources for people interested in developing mindfulness skills.
column16: Join students at the Teaching Kitchen at Student Health and Wellness. The Kitchen is home to the Hoo’s Cooking nutrition education and culinary skills classes, which are hands-on and led by fellow students, dietic interns, registered dieticians, professional chefs, and special guests.
column22: Looking for a couples therapist? The Gottman Institute, whose mission is to promote love and health in relationships, can guide you in finding the right provider for you and your partner in your area.

column4: Tutoring is for everyone! Housed on the second floor of Clemmons Library, the Math Collaborative Learning Center offers free assitance for various math courses.
column7: Search here to find Virginia's food stamp offices.
column8: Tools from UVA Community Credit Union
column10: Interested in learning about managing your money, investing, or even buying a house? Attend a free, one-hour seminar through UVA Community Credit Union.
column13: The CSC's mission is to promote flourishing at various stages of academic and career development. They offer yoga and mindfulness courses and so much more!
column14: Office of Health Promotion: Nutrition Services
column16: Interested in meeting with a registered dietician? You can schedule a nutrition consulation, which is grounded in a weight-neutral, health-forward approach, through the Office of Health Promotion.
column22: The National Healthy Marriage Resource Center offers a variety of resources to help married couples develop a healthier relationship.

column4: Virginia Workforce Connection is your gateway to employment and labor market information in Virginia.
column7: ReadyKids' Healthy Families program fosters nurturing family relationships for pregnant moms and safe environments for young children through regular home visiting.
column10: Call 211 for essential community services; 211 connects you to expert, caring help. Every call is completely confidential.
column13: Region Ten offers a variety of mental health services to people of all ages and backgrounds in the Charlottesville community and beyond.
column16: Check out Planned Parenthood for both informational resources on STDs, pregnancy, birth control options, etc., or to schedule an in-person or telehealth appointment with a medical professional.
column22: The Gottman Institute is focused on improving the well-being of marriages, families, and relationships through a research-based approach.

column4: Virginia Career Works is Virginia’s vital link between meaningful employment and growing businesses. Learn more about what they provide.
column7: REAL Dads coaches incarcerated fathers and fathers who want a stronger relationship with their children to have Responsible, Empowered, Available and Loving relationships with their families.
column8: Financial Wellness: Shifting from How Much You Have to How You Use It
column10: Learn about what financial wellness means and how to achieve it in your own life.
column13: If you're looking to get help beyond UVA, Partner for Mental Health is a community-focused resource that offers assistance to those experiencing mental health and addiction issues throughout Charlottesville.
column16: If you are pregnant or planning to become pregnant, Planned Parenthood offers specific resources on prenatal care, fertility treatments, pregnancy options, postpartum depression, and more!

column2: How to Handle Conflict in the Workplace
column4: Learn about some practical strategies you can use to handle conflict in the workplace with UCSD.
column5: Region Ten Child and Family Services
column7: The Child and Family Services center provides prevention, substance use and mental health services to the children and families in our community. The goals of these services are to help families build resiliency and keep children/youth safely in their homes and communities when possible.
column13: CAVA's mission is to provide an environment of support for individuals, couples, and families experiencing a wide range of life stressors in Charlottesville and Richmond.
column16: Thrive Central Virginia is a Christian non-profit that provides a 24/7 helpline and both walk-in or scheduled appointments regarding pregnancy testing, confidential options counseling, abortion information, ultrasounds, pregnancy support, post-abortion care and support, and more!

column4: By asking users to sign Commitment Contracts, stickK helps users define their goal (whatever it may be), acknowledge what it’ll take to accomplish it, and leverage the power of putting money on the line to turn that goal into a reality.
column5: Virginia Association of Community Service Boards - CSB Directory
column7: Use this Community Service Board directory to find locally-provided mental health services in your area.
column13: The UVA School of Nursing’s Compassionate Care Initiative (CCI) offers ongoing, weekly sessions on mindfulness meditation, mindful movement, yoga, and more. All drop-in sessions are free; no registration or previous experience required!
column16: Here are some simple ideas to build more activity into your day, tips to stick with it and free exercise plans to help you get going.

column2: A How-to Guide for Setting Better Goals
column4: Setting your own goals can be done in just four simple steps, outlined in this blog.
column7: VDSS is one of the largest Commonwealth agencies, partnering with 120 local departments of social services, along with faith-based and non-profit organizations, to promote the well-being of children and families statewide.
column13: UVA's Mindfulness Center offers weekly, free, 30-minute guided meditations followed by open discussion and questions. The meditation is led by Sam Green, PhD on Tuesdays from 5:30-6:30pm in McCleod Hall Room 2025.
column16: The American Heart Association has the tools and resources to get you on the right path to a healthier lifestyle.

column4: Don't hesitate to reach out to your Human Resources department when faced with workplace conflict; you are not alone.
column7: Hospice of the Piedmont is a community-based nonprofit hospice that has offered expert care and peaceful resolution to Virginia families for over 40 years.
column13: UVA's Mindfulness Center has created two videos, featuring Instructors Susan Stone and Susanna Williams, that discuss what mindfulness is and how it can benefit you.
column14: What Doctors Wish Patients Knew About Increasing Physical Activity
column16: This installment of the AMA’s "What Doctors WIsh Patient's Knew" discusses what patients need to know about physical activity.

column13: UVA's Mindfulness Center offers readings, videos, and transcripts with information on mindfulness and how to incorporate it into your lifestyle.
column16: The Virginia Family Nutrition Program (FNP) empowers Virginians to take charge of their health by eating healthy and living an active lifestyle. We teach limited resource households how to make healthier food choices and become better managers of available food resources for optimal health and growth.

column11: BIPOC Mental Health and Wellness Resources
column13: The Women's Center provides resources for individuals who identify as BIPOC and are exploring mental health and wellness on- and off- Grounds.
column16: Meet with a Registered Dietitian for individualized guidance on how to nourish yourself.

column11: LGBTQ+ Mental Health and Wellness Resources
column13: The Women's Center provides resources for individuals who identify as LGBTQ+ and are looking to explore mental health and wellness options on- and off- Grounds.
column16: Visit the Virginia Department of Health Family Planning site for information and services related to reproductive health.
